name: opencode-controller
tagline: ClawHub (79★): Control and operate Opencode via slash commands. Use this skill to manage sessions, select models, switch agents (plan/build
verdict: Solid marks across the board (security A, quality and hygiene B), but the whole flow leans on an external tool (Opencode start, selector, auth, plan execution) with no guidance for what to do if any step fails. No scope-exclusion language and no version tracking round out the minor gaps.

highlights.summary: Across a multi-step flow entirely dependent on an external tool (Opencode start, selector opening, authentication, plan execution), there is no mention anywhere of what to do if an
No 'NOT for' / exclusion / pointer-to-other-skill language appears anywhere in description or body, and the topic (general coding orchestration) is broad enough that misfire on adj
No version field in frontmatter, no changelog section, and no dated revision notes appear anywhere in the file.
